plan.title: Time announcements trigger in a continual loop

narratives.Description: Time announcements fire repeatedly in a tight/continual loop instead of once per interval boundary.
narratives.Overview: After #47 full-interval lookahead, miss-grace catch-up oscillates with the next reserved boundary: once scheduledBoundaryMs advances to the following mark, pumps re-prefer the still-in-grace missed mark and speak it immediately again.
narratives.Requirements:
FR-1: Each wall-clock interval boundary triggers at most once (catch-up or scheduled).
FR-2: Full-interval lookahead and miss-grace catch-up still work for background throttling.
FR-3: resync remaps future audio-clock schedules without re-speaking already-committed past boundaries.
NFR-1: Failing regression tests for the catch-up/lookahead oscillation (#62).
narratives.LockedDecisions: Fix in AnnounceEngine pump dedupe: commit watermark for spoken/scheduled boundaries that survives resync; do not regress to an earlier in-grace missed mark after reserving a later boundary.

references.title: Issue #62: Time announcements trigger in a continual loop
